reasoning: The United States has consistently updated and expanded its export controls on AI chips and related technologies on a near-annual basis in recent years (e.g., October 2022, October 2023, January 2025). While the recent January 2025 rules set a baseline, the Trump administration has signaled a strong "America First" stance on technology and a highly hawkish approach toward China. Modifying, tightening, or expanding restrictions on cloud AI and model weights—areas explicitly marked as emerging threats but currently under preliminary or newly implemented controls—is highly probable. Although the short time window (effective date between May 1 and Dec 31, 2025) presents a slight barrier to finalizing new, comprehensive rules, the momentum in BIS rulemaking on this issue and bipartisan support for techno-nationalist AI policies make further expansion likely within this timeframe.
